placements.reasoning: Placed 'escucha' (listen) in a replaceable cell near 'escuchar' (row 4, col 5) to group hearing-related actions.
Swapped with 'terminar' to place the more frequent first-person 'terminé' in its spot; 'terminar' moves to an empty cell.
Placed 'ayuda' (help) in a replaceable cell near 'ayudar' (row 0, col 4) for semantic grouping of assistance verbs.
Placed 'quiero' (I want) in a replaceable cell near 'querer' (row 3, col 4) and 'pensar' (row 6, col 4).
Placed 'no quiero' (I don't want) in a replaceable cell near other mental/volition verbs like 'pensar'.
Placed 'cenar' (to have dinner) near the 'cocinar y comer' folder to maintain the food-related cluster.
Placed 'sí' (yes) in a replaceable cell; it is a high-frequency functional word.
Placed 'dame' (give me) in a replaceable cell near 'dar' (row 1, col 8) and 'conseguir' (row 1, col 5).
Placed 'conocer' (to know/meet) near 'reunirse' (row 2, col 11) as they both relate to social interaction.
Placed 'no' in a replaceable cell near other communication and feeling words.
Swapped with 'detener' to place the more common imperative 'alto' (stop) in a primary position.
Placed 'voy a' (I'm going to) near the 'acciones de ir' folder to anchor it to movement.
Swapped with 'mirar' to place the imperative 'mira' (look) in the primary spot for sensory actions.
Swapped with 'mezclar' to place 'cortar' (cut) near 'cocinar' and 'comer' as a primary food prep action.
Swapped with 'me gusta' to place 'me encanta' (I love it) in a prominent position for expressing preference.
Swapped with 'guardar' to place 'juntar' (put together) near 'poner' and 'conseguir'.
Swapped with 'soy' to place 'estar' (to be) in a central verb position; 'soy' moves to an empty cell.
Swapped with 'tomar' to place the imperative 'toma' (take/here) in a high-frequency spot.
Swapped with 'dormir' to place 'descansar' (rest) near sleep and daily activities.
Placed 'desayunar' (to have breakfast) in a replaceable cell near 'comprar' and 'trabajar'.
Placed 'empezar' (start) near 'terminar' (finish) to create an antonym pair.
Swapped with 'esperar' to place the imperative 'espera' (wait) in the primary spot.
Swapped with 'pelear' to place 'pegar' (hit/stick) near other physical/conflict actions.
Swapped with 'odiar' to place 'no me gusta' (I don't like) near 'me gusta' and 'sentir'.

swaps.reasoning: 'terminé' is more frequently used in AAC than the infinitive 'terminar'.
'alto' is a more common imperative for 'stop' than 'detener'.
